doctrine: CPU-only, no GPU/CUDA dependencies. numpy + scipy + Pillow + ffmpeg only.
novelty_summary: Tier-aware splat dispatch (A/B/C) and per-splat phoxoidal density evaluation - neither feature exists in vanilla 3DGS renderers.
code_paths_exercised: front-to-back alpha compositing with early termination
per-splat SH evaluation (degrees 0..3, real basis) when sh_rest available
synthetic-germ fitter (per-splat curvature kappa_1, kappa_2 from k-NN local fit)
tier-aware dispatch (Tier A/B -> phoxoidal path, Tier C -> Gaussian fallback)

panels.name: ply_200k_512.png
panels.render_path: vanilla Gaussian (PLY has no tier labels; defaults to Tier C everywhere)
panels.honesty_caveat: Reference: standard 3DGS render with no tier dispatch.

panels.name: v28_phox_200k_gaussian.png
panels.honesty_caveat: Tier dispatch wired in but every tier dispatched to the Gaussian rasterizer for a clean baseline.

panels.name: v28_phox_200k_phoxoidal_strong.png
panels.render_path: Tier A/B -> phoxoidal density (LAMBDA=0.5 modulation), Tier C -> Gaussian
panels.honesty_caveat: Synthetic curvature germs auto-fitted from each splat's 8-NN local neighborhood (real germ data not yet in .3dphox format). Phoxoidal density uses a screen-space approximation; faithful per-pixel Newton solve is v0.3 work.

panels.name: v28_tier_overlay_200k.png
panels.render_path: tier-color override (Tier A=red, B=green, C=blue) instead of SH RGB
panels.honesty_caveat: Diagnostic overlay showing which spatial regions belong to which tier. The red/green concentrations land on the actual car body surface; blue is the ambient halo. Visualizes the project's spatial doctrine in one image.

open_followups: Decode v28's VQ-encoded SH so the v28 renders carry proper view-dependent color (only DC was used here).
Replace the screen-space phoxoidal approximation with a faithful per-pixel closest-point Newton solve for higher-fidelity germs.
Add cusp/twist germ basis terms (Pearcey-class) and re-fit; will reveal genuinely non-bell-shape phoxoid blobs.
Tier B exact-residual correction lookup from v28's archive container.
Try side/three-quarter camera angles where the halo doesn't fully cover the car body at full splat density.
